entity.name: Tağmaç Çankaya
entity.description: Independent maintainer in Lefkoşa, Cyprus (Republic of Cyprus / EU citizen). I build small, commons-aligned AGPLv3 tools for solo operators and small teams: Echo Toolkit (discovery), Cairn (token-efficient multi-agent coordination), and Khashif. A consistent discipline across fifteen years of civic, environmental, and digital work: efficiency through configuration, not brute force; ship something concrete and let it endure.

projects.name: Cairn
projects.description: A free AGPLv3 coordination layer that lets multiple AI agents work together without burning the token budget, by coordinating through a passive shared blackboard and minimal per-agent context instead of conversation. Existing tooling is reactive (budget caps, observability); Cairn is preventive: it removes the unbounded-conversation structure that causes context and cost to explode. Measured on a controlled A/B (identical task, same model, only the context architecture changed): 54% fewer input tokens, 65% lower cost. The benchmark harness is published so the saving is reproducible. Framework-agnostic; composes with LangGraph and CrewAI. Also submitted to NLnet NGI Zero Commons (code 2026-06-385); FLOSS/fund support would fund maintenance, framework adapters, and the public benchmark.

channels.description: EU (Cyprus) bank transfer. Account details provided on request to the maintainer.

plans.name: Cairn v1 development grant
plans.description: Fund 4 months of solo engineering: minimal-context core, stigmergic coordination, gardener orchestrator plus reproducible benchmark harness, framework adapters and docs. Milestone-deliverable.
